title: Claim spine
description: Editorial narrative discipline borrowed from Codex Presentations skill Phase 1 (Narrative Spine). Encodes: every non-exempt spread carries a CLAIM (a conclusion, not a topic label), a proof object, and a support note. Lives as the `claim_spine` block in library/articles/<slug>.yaml. May also be persisted standalone at output/<slug>/claim_spine.json. Editorial copy (title/lead/body/captions) stays in spread_copy[]; this schema validates the narrative spine that should sharpen that copy.

thesis.description: What this issue proves. One sentence. Source-backable when it makes factual claims.

audience.description: Who this issue is for. Editorial register follows from this.

noun_swap_test_notes.description: Author confirms titles fail the noun-swap test: if you swap the subject's name (Luna -> Naipi -> Anyone), titles should stop making sense. Cite which spreads were sharpened.

exempt.description: True only for spreads that legitimately lack a claim (toc, colophon). When true, claim_title/proof_object/kicker are not required and exempt_reason must be provided.

kicker.description: 1-3 words naming the role. e.g. 'Chapter 01', 'EXPANSION DRIVERS', 'CODA'. Stable when claim_title is rewritten.

claim_title.description: A CONCLUSION, not a topic label. Bad: 'Departure'. Good: 'Four billion years of silence broke under a paw.' Must fail the noun-swap test.

support_note.description: Concise, factual, source-backed. The reader's 'why is this claim true' answer.

omitted_alternatives.description: Optional audit trail: candidate claim_titles considered and rejected, with reason.

kind.description: What kind of dominant evidence carries the claim on this spread.

ref.description: Reference to the proof: image_slot id (e.g. 'feature_hero'), image_slot pattern (e.g. 'portrait_wall.[1-6]'), region id, or text field name.

why_carries_claim.description: One line on why this object proves the claim.
